title: conium maculatum subsp. viride
intro: Conium maculatum subsp. viride, also known as the green hemlock, is a subspecies of the toxic Conium maculatum plant, native to Europe and Western Asia. Although the plant itself is toxic due to the presence of coniine, a naturally occurring alkaloid, it has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Conium maculatum subsp. viride include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can be beneficial for relieving pain and muscle spasms. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas or tinctures, and when used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional, it may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as support the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis.
